programmes.show_description: The Gift of Promise: When a businesswoman is bludgeoned to death, it looks like a case of blackmail gone wrong but there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case.
Vacancy for Inspector: Enduring British sitcom. Jack gets promoted to the role of inspector as Stan gets more than he bargained for when he invites a new conductor home for tea.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Thicker Than Water: Comedy series about rival antique dealers. Simon reveals a secret surrounding his birth to Oliver - and learns of an even stranger mystery in return.
Bye Bye Blakey: Classic sitcom about a bus driver and his conductor pal. Stan and Jack eavesdrop on a medical Blakey is having, but they end up jumping to the wrong conclusions.
Viv and Bob are stunned by Louise's suggestion she buy their businesses. Gennie worries she'll never be a proper Dingle, and Laurel makes progress with Arthur.
Debbie gets jealous when Eli opts to spend time with Gennie rather than her. Viv tells Louise she'll sell her the business, and Katie informs Gray and Perdy she wants a Caesarean.
Liam leaves for work as a distraught Maria packs her bag for the hospital, unable to find a way of telling him about the baby. Meanwhile, Jack learns Paul has been charged.
Maria continues to keep Liam in the dark as she heads back to the Street. Bill lets slip to a perplexed Gail about Audrey meeting Ted, and Liz refuses to go on Vernon's cruise.
Widows and Orphans: There is plenty on Frost's plate when senior citizens are targeted in a string of vicious attacks, but at least he can call on his new female sergeant for help.
Wall of Silence: Nick investigates an apparent crime wave in Aidensfield after a mysterious spate of burglaries, and the Assistant Chief Constable is due to visit Lord Ashfordly's.
Missing: Drama series set in the 1960s. The annual funfair comes to Aidensfield and causes much excitement - but some ill feeling too - before a five-year-old boy goes missing.
Sauce for the Goose: Barnaby and Scott delve into the murder of a visitor to a relish factory and discover a web of secrets and lies within the family that own it.
Debbie reveals Gennie's scheme to Pollard as jealousy about Eli's friendship with the girl overwhelms her. Lexi asks Donald for her job back and Donna tells Marlon to get a job.
Donald tells Carl about his plans to retire and asks him to be managing director of the company. Diane offers Marlon his old job back and Eli asks Gennie out for lunch.
Claire discovers the extent of the family's financial difficulties when Ashley admits to using their own money to prop up the business. Audrey makes a disturbing discovery.
Audrey's shocking discovery prompts Maria to confess her distressing secret, Marcus is suspicious when Sean returns early from holiday and Chesney is upset by Les's news.
Speed Kills: Drama series. A break-in at the surgery heralds the beginning of a serious investigation for Nick, and Kate discovers the cause of a patient's previous miscarriage.
Riders of the Storm: Nick hears that a violent criminal is to be released from prison and may be heading his way, and a snowstorm leaves Kate cut off with a heavily pregnant woman.
Find Them Dead: Crime drama. A high-stakes criminal trial of a ruthless Brighton drug lord and a puzzling double murder investigation push Grace's team to the very brink.
The Electric Vendetta: Feature-length drama. A ufologist claims extra-terrestrial involvement when a naked and shaved man is found in a crop circle with puncture wounds on his back.
Generation of Vipers: Lewis and Hathaway are drawn into a world of virtual bullying - but very real murder - when a professor is found dead after a dating video is leaked online.
A Thin Time: Sitcom about a two-man bus crew. Arthur is attracted to Beryl the clippie, but Beryl has only two interests in life - athletic men and those with a good head of hair.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
Kung Who: Comedy series about two rival antique dealers. Oliver accepts Simon's challenge to take up judo - and soon regrets his decision.
The Prize: Classic sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Everyone wants to go when Mum wins a holiday for two to the Costa Brava.
Charity Begins at Home: Sitcom. Mike urges his parents to spend a weekend living on bread and water and giving the money saved on food to charity, but Sid finds it quite difficult.
Wives and Lovers: Classic '70s sitcom with Sid James. Jean hatches a plan to catch Sid out when she gets worried about the many flirty women that he meets in his line of work.
I'm Not Jealous, I'll Kill Him!: Classic sitcom. An old flame from Jean's youth comes back into her life and asks her out to dinner - Sid is surprisingly fine with this... or is he?
The Unkindest Cut of All: Domestic sitcom spin-off from the show Man About the House. George is ordered to watch his table manners when Mildred's sister comes round for dinner.
Comedy spin-off film sequel starring Harry H Corbett and Wilfrid Brambell, 1973. Albert must die when things get very bad at the Steptoes' Shepherd's Bush rag-and-bone business.

programmes.show_name: Carry On... Don't Lose Your Head
programmes.show_description: Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Jim Dale, 1967. Two English noblemen decide to rescue their French counterparts from the revolting masses and Madame Guillotine.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1973. The town council of a seaside resort are persuaded to hold a beauty contest with chaotic results.
Bawdy British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1974. 1750 - a newly-formed police force attempt to catch Dick Turpin, the most notorious of all highwaymen.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Kenneth Williams, 1972. A package holiday on the island of Elsbels turns into a hilarious adventure rife with innuendo and gags.
Classic British comedy film starring Sid James and Barbara Windsor, 1972. A gang plans to raid a maternity hospital in order to steal a gigantic stock of birth control pills.
Driving Mr McLynn: Comedy-drama series. Martin is shocked when Louisa reveals that she wants to be the school headteacher again, and Pauline worries about Al's secretive behaviour.
The Departed: Comedy-drama series. A visit for Martin to London is disturbed on the way back home by the death of a local sheep farmer whose widow starts to see visions of him.
Dark Autumn: Feature-length drama. DCI Barnaby uncovers a hotbed of adultery within a remote hamlet when a postman is brutally murdered - can a community WPC help find the killer?
